question_text: Which statement correctly compares a joule and a watt?

options.label: A
options.text: A joule is a unit of power and a watt is a unit of energy

options.label: B
options.text: A joule is a unit of energy and a watt is a unit of power

options.label: C
options.text: A joule and a watt are both units of energy

options.label: D
options.text: A joule and a watt are both units of power

correct_answer: B) A joule is a unit of energy and a watt is a unit of power
explanation: A joule (J) measures the amount of energy transferred, while a watt (W) measures the rate at which energy is transferred, which is power. One watt equals one joule per second.
